title: Systematic Root-Cause Categorization of Real-World Errors
description: Categorization of all 10 real-world errors (4 FPs + 6 FNs) from the 56-benchmark Suite C evaluation.
methodology: Each error is classified into one of four root-cause categories: (1) formalization gap — incorrect or incomplete PyTorch operation semantics; (2) graph extraction failure — AST parsing cannot extract the required data flow; (3) coverage-boundary artifact — operations handled via shape-preserving assumption rather than exact modeling; (4) out-of-fragment pattern — the program uses constructs outside F_TensorGuard.

false_positives.description: BERT encoder with embedding, multi-head attention, FFN, and pooler. The tool reports a spurious shape error because the graph extractor cannot track that nn.MultiheadAttention preserves the embed_dim through its internal Q/K/V projection and output projection. The 87% coverage reflects unmodeled attention internals.
false_positives.proposed_fix: Add MultiheadAttention as a first-class operation with shape transfer function: input (B, seq, embed_dim) → output (B, seq, embed_dim).

false_positives.description: DETR-style detector with complex reshape: x.permute(0,2,3,1).reshape(x.size(0),-1,256). The AST extractor does not track permute+reshape composition, leading to a spurious shape mismatch at the cross-attention step. Coverage 83% reflects unmodeled permute semantics.
false_positives.proposed_fix: Add permute as a tracked operation with explicit dimension reordering semantics.

false_positives.description: Squeeze-and-Excitation block with adaptive pooling → FC → reshape to (B,C,1,1) → element-wise multiply. Despite 100% coverage, the tool flags a false positive because broadcast semantics for the channel attention gating (x * se where se has trivial spatial dims) are not correctly modeled for 4D tensors with singleton dimensions.
false_positives.proposed_fix: Extend broadcast compatibility checking to handle singleton dimension broadcasting in 4D tensors: (B,C,H,W) * (B,C,1,1) should be valid.

false_positives.description: U-Net with skip connections using torch.cat([decoder_out, encoder_out], dim=1). The tool's formalization of concatenation does not correctly compute the output channel dimension when followed by a Conv2d that expects the concatenated channel count. The tool flags a spurious shape mismatch at the post-concat convolution.
false_positives.proposed_fix: Improve torch.cat formalization to compute exact output dimensions along the concatenation axis and propagate to subsequent layers.

false_negatives.description: Attention scaling with learned scale parameter creating wrong broadcasting: (B,seq,seq) * (B,seq,1). The shapes are broadcast-compatible per PyTorch rules, but the operation is semantically incorrect. This is outside F_TensorGuard because the tool checks shape compatibility, not semantic correctness of broadcasts. Detecting this requires intent-aware analysis.
false_negatives.proposed_fix: This class of bug (semantically incorrect but shape-compatible broadcasts) is fundamentally outside the scope of shape verification. Would require user-supplied semantic constraints.

false_negatives.description: BERT pooler nn.Linear(512, 768) receives cls_token with dim 768. The tool does not propagate dimensions through MultiheadAttention, so it cannot verify that the attention output dimension (768) mismatches the pooler input dimension (512).
false_negatives.proposed_fix: Same as bert_encoder_correct FP fix: model MultiheadAttention output dimension propagation.

false_negatives.description: torch.cat([text_feat, img_feat], dim=1) where text_feat is (B,128) and img_feat is (B,64) produces (B,192), but subsequent Linear expects 256. The tool does not track concatenation output dimensions for 2D tensors.
false_negatives.proposed_fix: Extend concatenation dimension tracking to 2D tensors (not just 4D spatial tensors).

false_negatives.description: FIXED: nn.Flatten(start_dim=0) now correctly tracked. The graph extractor extracts the start_dim parameter and models its effect on shape.
false_negatives.proposed_fix: IMPLEMENTED: Parse start_dim parameter of nn.Flatten and propagate correctly.

false_negatives.description: FIXED: nn.MultiheadAttention now validated as first-class layer. The propagator checks that the input last dimension matches embed_dim.
false_negatives.proposed_fix: IMPLEMENTED: MultiheadAttention propagator with embed_dim validation.

false_negatives.description: U-Net decoder Conv2d(64, 64) receives 128-channel input after skip connection concatenation. The tool tracks torch.cat dimension sums correctly, but the graph extractor does not connect the concat result through variable reassignments in complex forward() methods.
false_negatives.proposed_fix: Requires improved graph extraction for variable reassignment tracking through multi-step operations.

summary.coverage_correlation: Of the 4 FPs, 2 have reduced coverage (0.867, 0.833) indicating coverage-correlated false alarms, while 2 have full coverage indicating formalization gaps. Of the 4 remaining FNs, all have 100% coverage — these are graph extraction failures or formalization gaps in complex forward() patterns (variable reassignment, tuple unpacking, tensor slicing).
summary.key_insight: After fixing MultiheadAttention validation and nn.Flatten start_dim tracking, F1 improved from 0.857 to 0.889. The remaining 8 errors are dominated by graph extraction limitations in complex forward() patterns: tuple unpacking from MHA (a, _ = self.attn(x, x, x)), tensor slicing (x[:, :300]), and multi-step variable reassignment chains. These require deeper AST analysis improvements.
summary.f_tensorguard_boundary: Only 1 of 8 remaining errors (attention_scale_broadcast_bug) represents a fundamental limitation of the F_TensorGuard fragment. The remaining 7 are engineering gaps in graph extraction that can be incrementally addressed.
